message.content: I changed the rate limit but production still shows the old value. How does deploy even work here?

content.text: Let me check the CI workflows to see what a push does.

deploy-dev.yml runs on push to the dev branch: it runs the Go test gate, builds an image, pushes it to ECR, then deploys to the EC2 instance via AWS SSM send-command. So a push to dev IS the deploy — no separate step. It takes about 5 minutes end to end.

message.content: OK but I already pushed and the rate limit didn't change. Is it env?

content.text: Yes. The service reads RATE_LIMIT from an env file. On the box the app runs via docker compose out of /opt/acme-gateway, and the environment lives in /opt/acme-gateway/.env. That compose file and .env are NOT synced from the repo, so a code push never touches them — runtime env changes have to be applied on the box, not committed.

message.content: how do I get on the box? I don't have the ssh key

content.text: You don't need it. SSH on this instance is publickey-only and none of the local keys are authorized. Use AWS SSM Session Manager instead — the instance shows Online in describe-instance-information, so SSM send-command and start-session both work for admin and env changes.

message.content: btw my laptop only has aws cli v1, the v2-only flags don't work for me. anyway, edited the .env and restarted, it works now

content.text: Good. To summarize the deploy model: push to dev = full CI build + SSM deploy (~5 min); runtime config lives in /opt/acme-gateway/.env on the box and is not in the repo; reach the box via SSM, not SSH.
